## What We Were Doing
- Task: Overhaul content based on CMU 17-445 comparison, de-AI the prose, add dark mode, restructure lecture page layout
- Progress:
  - [x] CMU 17-445 research + vault notes review → identified 8 substantive gaps
  - [x] L1: Added systems thinking + "planning for mistakes" framing, renamed to "From Notebook to Production Service"
  - [x] L2: Expanded from "pick best model" to "navigate quality tradeoffs", require written justification for model choice
  - [x] L3: Minor prose tightening
  - [x] L4: Added data quality vs drift distinction, feedback loops, A/B testing, broader security
  - [x] L5: Connected safety back to L1 thesis, added adversarial inputs/data poisoning/model extraction
  - [x] Overview: Replaced safety section (Wrong Predictions + Feedback Loops instead of Hallucination Risk + Prompt Injection)
  - [x] Humanizer pass on all prose (removed significance inflation, -ing analyses, negative parallelisms, rule-of-three, promotional language)
  - [x] Lecture page restructure: deliverable → what you'll build → concept → topics → from classroom to full scale
  - [x] Section renames: "Hands-On" → "What You'll Build", "How Big Tech Does This" → "From Classroom to Full Scale"
  - [x] Schedule table: removed fixed widths, added min-width floors on deliverable/tools columns
  - [x] Dark mode: CSS custom properties, auto system preference detection, toggle button in sidebar header, localStorage persistence
  - [x] Concept section: removed max-w-2xl constraint (full width now)
  - [x] MDX frontmatter reordered to match page render order (all 5 lectures + overview)
  - [ ] User visual verification of live site — nav, dark mode toggle, mermaid rendering, section order

## Decisions
- MDX content collections over JSON → better writing experience for prose + structured frontmatter
- `<base href="/ucla-lectures/">` + relative links for gh-pages nav
- Dark mode uses CSS custom properties (not Tailwind `dark:` classes) for full control
- Theme toggle in sidebar header (not floating button) — consistent location, mobile-friendly
- Section order on lecture pages: deliverable → what you'll build → concept → topics → scale
- CMU comparison drove content changes but NOT structure — we kept the 5-lecture arc unchanged
- L5 hybrid ML+LLM architecture remains the key differentiator from other MLOps courses
- `<base href>` is hardcoded — local dev nav links may resolve incorrectly (known issue, not yet addressed)
